explanatory_strength.rationale: Filings still support a hard-to-replicate platform: AWS switching costs, Prime density, logistics scale, and custom silicon. Q1 2026 AWS +28% with 37.7% segment margin matches the reinvestment narrative. The research loop did not weaken the moat mechanism; it only clarified that normalized owner-cash timing, not competitive advantage, is the open question.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Round-two research confirmed the contract owner-earnings row ($7.7B) cannot be reconciled to $148.5B TTM operating cash flow and that committee pricing must use the Lawrence normalized bridge (~$5.35 per share). That closes one falsifier but leaves the decision-critical capex normalization bridge unresolved: ~$90B sustainable capex, incremental AI datacenter ROIC, and inflection timing remain [Assumption] with zero management_facts and stale filing_facts QA.

downside_control.rationale: Operating cash flow scale and $86.8B cash limit insolvency risk, but equity downside is real if capex stays near $151B without normalized free cash flow per share inflecting or if the multiple compresses. Capex-stress trough free cash flow is roughly $0.11 per share. Partial dhando, not a cheap-enough mispriced asset.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Lawrence total synthesis remains 3.68% per year at ~$247 on $5.35 per share normalized owner cash, well below a ~15% portfolio hurdle. Contract reinvestment DCF at $11.64 per share base is disqualified; bull 7.2% and AI sensitivity 16.2% still depend on capex normalization assumptions the packet cannot filing-verify.

claims.claim: Research response confirmed valuation_contract.json labels owner earnings at $7,695M from the operating-cash-flow filing tag while evidence reconciliation cites $148.5B TTM operating cash flow; committee pricing must use the Lawrence normalized bridge (~$58.5B owner cash, ~$5.35 per share), not the contract proof row.

TTM operating cash flow was $148.5B while productive-asset capex was ~$151B, leaving trailing free cash flow near $1.2B; no filing- or management-grounded bridge to a ~$90B sustainable mid-cycle capex run rate exists in the frozen packet.
Q1 2026 AWS net sales were $37.6B (+28% YoY) with segment operating income margin 37.7%, confirming the cloud profit engine independent of capex-timing uncertainty.

strongest_counter_explanation: The market is rationally paying for a scarce AI infrastructure platform: hyperscaler capex is offensive reinvestment that widens AWS and custom-silicon moats, and normalized owner cash will inflect toward $9+ per share faster than skeptics assume, making a low-single-digit filing IRR misleading at today's price.
most_important_missing_fact: A filing- or management-grounded bridge from current ~$151B productive-asset capex to a sustainable mid-cycle run rate (~$90B) with observable incremental after-tax return on AI and datacenter capital, including timing of free cash flow per share inflection.
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with operating cash flow growth below 5% YoY while capex remains above $140B
AWS revenue growth decelerates below 15% with segment operating margin falling below 30%
Management discloses sustainable capex above $120B with no path to normalized owner cash above $6 per share within three years
specialist_findings: Round-two research answered the contract-proof falsifier: the owner-earnings input is filing-inconsistent and the automated DCF base ($11.64 per share) must be ignored for committee pricing. Within the Munger power zone for moat and inversion review; competitive advantage looks stable and multi-sourced, but the lollapalooza of AI capex plus optionality pricing remains a classic great-business-wrong-price setup. Overlap controls are clean (single additive contract component; four distinct overlap keys in evidence reconciliation). Advertising (> $70B TTM) and Anthropic marks stay embedded assumptions, not independently risked options. Invert: if capex never normalizes, you own a high-quality cash generator that never pays you. Watch for capex discipline and a filing-grounded normalization bridge; do not approve at spot.
